demographics.living_situation: lives with their partner; reports feeling unsafe at home

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My entire body is screaming in pain, I can't catch my breath, and I am terrified to leave this house because of what might happen if I ask for help.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain and the panic have been building over the last couple of days after a bad argument at home and tonight it became unbearable; the patient asked to be seen alone and does not want to go to a hospital.

symptom_narrative.onset: worsening over the past 48 hours, following an argument at home; became severe tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: about two days, now at its worst
symptom_narrative.character: widespread aching, burning pain all over the body, especially the neck, shoulders and back; also a deep pain low in the belly/pelvis; racing heart and fast, shallow breathing
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is the worst it has ever felt and it is intolerable right now
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: everywhere — neck, shoulders, back, and low in the belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it flared, comes in waves that get worse with fear or noise
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; sitting quietly helps the breathing a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any sudden noise, thinking about the relationship, or the idea of having to ask anyone for help
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling on edge and jumpy, heart pounding, breathless, shaky, and a strong urge to keep checking the front window
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: says the body pain is long-standing and flares under stress, but has never been this severe
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot settle, cannot do basic tasks, and cannot bring themselves to leave the house

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing widespread body pain that flares when stressed

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with their partner and describes the home situation as frightening and hard to leave; feels financially tied to the relationship; is afraid that asking for help or involving authorities could make things worse or lead to being judged; has quietly packed a small bag that is sitting by the door.

communication_style.typical_message_style: frightened and guarded; apologises frequently, downplays the partner's behaviour, and repeatedly seeks reassurance that no one will call the police

reluctance_topics: details of the relationship and what happens at home
any involvement of police or authorities
going to a hospital or emergency department

access_barriers.financial: feels financially entangled and unable to safely separate
access_barriers.other: fears institutional involvement and being stereotyped; struggles to find culturally appropriate, confidential support and safe housing

objective_data_offered.value: my heart is pounding really fast and won't slow down
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own sensation, not a measured rate

objective_data_offered.value: I'm breathing really fast and can't seem to catch my breath
I don't see any new bruises or cuts on me right now
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-report only; cannot exclude concealed or internal injury
